compiled_state.title: Brainstorm defers feasibility-recommended spike instead of executing it before spec finalization
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Summary

During brainstorm for the Ticket System Boundary Consolidation epic, the feasibility reviewer identified a critical integration risk (score 2/5) with the Jira issue properties endpoint: ACLI has no properties subcommand, requiring a new direct HTTP code path that breaks the established ACLI-only pattern. The reviewer explicitly recommended: 'Add a spike story to validate the endpoint before building the full bridge pipeline.'

The brainstorm skill's response was to note the finding and suggest the spike could happen within the epic — deferring the spike to implementation time rather than resolving it during brainstorm. This means the epic spec was heading toward approval with an unvalidated integration assumption.

## Expected Behavior

When the feasibility reviewer recommends a spike to validate an integration assumption, the brainstorm skill should either:
1. Execute the spike (or a lightweight verification) during brainstorm before finalizing the spec
2. Re-enter the Phase 1 understanding loop with the feasibility gap (per the FEASIBILITY_GAP handler)
3. Present the unresolved risk explicitly at the approval gate with the spike as a prerequisite

## Actual Behavior

The brainstorm skill acknowledged the integration_risk=2 finding and the spike recommendation but continued toward spec revision and approval without executing or gating on the spike. The user had to explicitly request the spike be run first.

## Suggested Fix

When the feasibility reviewer scores integration_risk below 3 AND recommends a spike or validation step, the brainstorm skill should:
1. Check if the spike can be executed within the brainstorm session (e.g., running a CLI help command, checking API docs, testing an endpoint)
2. If executable: run it and incorporate findings into the spec before the approval gate
3. If not executable within session: annotate the spec with a FEASIBILITY_GAP section and trigger the FEASIBILITY_GAP handler (re-enter understanding loop or escalate to user)

The current flow treats feasibility findings as informational rather than gating, which undermines the purpose of the feasibility reviewer.

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral (cluster: fc9b-1d9a + b1ff-fa71). Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Investigation (bot-psychologist): Taxonomy #6 Termination Awareness Failure. Root cause: FEASIBILITY_GAP handler has no spike-response branch — only re-enters Phase 1 loop, never gates on spike execution. Fix: add spike-check step 3 to handler + add FEASIBILITY_GAP prerequisite to approval gate HARD-GATE checklist.
comments.author: Test
